subject: Chemistry
depth_profile: The provided pages focus on laboratory investigations rather than theoretical learning objectives for this topic. For buffer solutions, the scope includes preparing a buffer solution by selecting appropriate buffer components, determining the concentrations needed to meet the target pH and buffer capacity, selecting the reagents needed to prepare the solution, and characterizing the resulting solutions to confirm that they meet the targets.
assessment_notes: Many questions on the AP Exam are written in an experimental context. Students should be able to describe how to collect data, reduce data to form conclusions, and identify and discuss relevant sources of error. Students must report recorded data and quantitative conclusions with appropriate precision (i.e., significant figures) and understand how changes in the design of experiments would impact the validity and accuracy of their results.
spec_source_citation: AP Chemistry Course and Exam Description (College Board)
